stories.title: The secret drone operation that’s changing the Ukraine war
stories.summary: UNDISCLOSED LOCATION, Ukraine — At a secret warehouse, in the darkness of night, masked specialists from Ukraine’s military intelligence service GUR drill and hammer as they assemble a line of 15-foot-long aircraft. These are Ukrainian Liutyi long-range kamikaze drones, carrying explosive payloads of up to 150 pounds and capable of traveling nearly 1,300 miles. Later that night, they will be launched toward targets inside Russia. “They are now our most important card in this war,” says a Ukraini

stories.title: The political fight behind Washington’s export controls on Anthropic
stories.summary: Aligning your message with the White House is standard political practice for tech companies trying to stay on the right side of fast-changing AI policies in U.S. President Donald Trump’s Washington. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has not played that game. Amodei has over the last few months repeatedly diverged from White House messaging in ways that set him apart from other Big Tech CEOs. He clashed with U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth over artificial intelligence’s battlefield role. He warned

stories.title: Trump is tired of arming allies. This country is stepping up.
stories.summary: The United States can’t be on the hook anymore for protecting the world, the president lectured. “The defense of freedom is everybody’s business — not just America’s business,” he said in a White House address. “And it is particularly the responsibility of the people whose freedom is threatened.” What feels like a quintessentially Trumpian speech was delivered by Richard Nixon in 1969. Long before Donald Trump was unnerving Europe by scaling back U.S. security commitments to the continent, Nixon

stories.title: Ukraine officials return Polish honors after Zelenskyy stripped of award
stories.summary: A decision to strip Ukrainian leader Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor benefited Moscow, Ukrainian officials said. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia's war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.title: Even in this age of what Mark Carney calls global rupture, do not despair: there is still hope for international law | Nathalie Tocci
stories.summary: Developments in Ukraine and Iran show that the military superpowers are not getting it all their own way Our age of what Mark Carney called global rupture is also often described as following the “ law of the jungle ”, in which the strong do what they can and the weak suffer what they must, with international law shattered and multilateral organisations hollowed out. Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Israel’s onslaught on Gaza, and the US and Israel’s attacks on Iran and Lebanon seem to confirm this

stories.title: ‘It’s Russian roulette’: alarm as Europe backs critical minerals mines in water-stressed regions
stories.summary: Exclusive: European Commission planning to rewrite key law to allow water-intensive mines in regions suffering from drought The European Commission plans to rewrite the EU’s flagship water protection law to speed up the development of critical minerals mines, despite many being located in drying and water-stressed regions, analysis has found. Mining is a water-intensive industry, requiring large volumes of water for ore processing, dust suppression, waste management and mine dewatering. While mo

stories.title: Poland's president strips Zelenskyy of top honor
stories.summary: Karol Nawrocki's decision to strip Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest honor is likely to spark a severe diplomatic crisis between Poland and Ukraine.

stories.title: Zelensky stripped of highest Polish honour over WW2 name of army unit
stories.summary: Ukraine has denounced the move, calling it a "strategic mistake" and "disrespectful".

stories.title: Tűz ütött ki Dominika egyik luxusszállodájában, 1700 embert kellett kimenekíteni
stories.summary: Egy olasz nő meghalt füstmérgezés miatt. A nádtető miatt villámgyorsan terjedtek a lángok.

stories.title: Pörgetnék az ukrán EU-csatlakozást Brüsszelben, de Magyar Péter megfúrta a sietséget
stories.summary: A megkérdezett tagállami vezetők így is örültek az új magyar miniszterelnök érkezésének, hiszen jóváhagyta a csatlakozási tárgyalások folytatását, amit Orbánék két éve blokkoltak.

stories.title: A Fidesz szerint Magyar Péter sógora titkos paktumot kötött az egyik alkotmánybíróval
stories.summary: A párt szerint ezért nem engedik napirendre Sulyok Tamás alkotmánybírósági beadványát. A magyar alkotmányosság egyik legszégyenteljesebb napjának nevezték a péntekit.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid lezárná a Közlekedési Múzeum debreceni költözésének abszurd tervét
stories.summary: Magyar Péterrel nyitották meg a Közlekedési Múzeum új kiállítását az Északi Járműjavítóban. A miniszterelnök szerint Vitézy vezetésével új lendületet kaphat a közlekedés.

stories.title: Majka: Viszlát Mandiner, Pesti Srácok, Megafon! Ott rohadjatok meg, ahol vagytok!
stories.summary: Huszonharmadszor lépett fel a Budapest Parkban Majka, a Csurran, cseppen című dalának végén elköszönt Bindzsisztántól is.

stories.title: „Az egyetlen hátránya, hogy nem kapok érte jogdíjat” – 50 éve lőtte Panenka a legendás tizenegyesét
stories.summary: A csehszlovák középpályás az 1976-os Európa-bajnoki döntő legfontosabb pillanatában vált halhatatlanná.

stories.title: Szaniszló Judit: A mellettünk elsuhanó élet
stories.summary: „Felesleges az újság, az ablakon kopogva zümmögő legyet végül kézzel nyomja oda az üveghez a fehér cérnafüggönnyel együtt. Pattan a kitin, csend lesz.” E heti novellánk egy fiktív háború hátországában játszódik.

stories.title: Megérkezett az ukránok válasza arra, hogy a lengyel elnök visszavonta Zelenszkij kitüntetését
stories.summary: Az ukrán elnöki iroda vezetője és a varsói ukrán nagykövet lemondott a korábban nekik adományozott lengyel tiszti keresztről.

stories.title: Jól hangzik! – reagálta Trump arra, hogy Hitlerhez, Maóhoz és Sztálinhoz hasonlították
stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök újraosztott egy szöveget, amiben amellett érvelt a szerző, hogy Trump még a félelmet keltő történelmi személyiségeknél is befolyásosabb.

stories.title: Mégsem nyújtják be jövő héten a Nemzeti Vagyonvisszaszerzési és Védelmi Hivatalról szóló javaslatot
stories.summary: Magyar Péter azt mondta, előtte társadalmi egyeztetésre bocsátják a törvénytervezetet, de reméli, hogy szeptember elején fel tud állni a hivatal.

stories.title: Nem hagyhatja el az országot a spanyol miniszterelnök korrupcióval vádolt felesége
stories.summary: Begoña Gómeznek az útlevelét is le kellett adnia, a tárgyalás időpontját még nem tűzték ki.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt a tatabányai kisfiú haláláról: Nem engedhetjük meg magunknak, hogy ezekből az esetekből ne tanuljunk
stories.summary: Szerinte az egészségügyben Magyarországon sokszor nem a tanulás lett a reflex, hanem a szőnyeg alá söprés és az egyének hibáztatása.

stories.title: Nyolc évre ítélték a tömegmészárlással fenyegetőző, Magyarországon élő norvég férfit
stories.summary: A férfi korábban arról is beszélt, hogy túl akarja szárnyalni a norvég tömeggyilkos, Anders Breivik mészárlását, aki 77 embert ölt meg 2011-ben.

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
stories.summary: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: Revealed: Brexit voting areas have seen faster growth in foreign workers since EU referendum
stories.summary: Guardian investigation also finds same areas experienced relative decline over same period Leave-voting areas have seen faster relative growth in foreign workers since the Brexit referendum, a Guardian investigation has found. Data analysis suggests that the decade since the Brexit vote may not have matched the expectations of many Leave supporters, showing their local areas have also become relatively more deprived over the same period. Continue reading...

stories.title: Zelenskyy gives Belarus a week to remove relay stations helping Russia
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has given Belarus a one-week ultimatum to remove relay equipment on its territory that he says is helping Russia attack Ukraine, warning that Kyiv will otherwise intervene itself to halt the transmissions. In a social media post on Friday night, Zelenskyy directly urged his Belarusian counterpart, Alexander Lukashenko, to “remove that equipment.” “I think a week is enough for him to do that. … If he doesn’t do it, we will,” Zelenskyy said. Zelenskyy added

stories.title: Israel continues strikes in Lebanon despite ceasefire with Hezbollah
stories.summary: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed several people amid overnight exchanges of fire between Israeli forces and Iran-backed Hezbollah. According to the Lebanese Civil Defense , 16 people were killed and 12 wounded in Israeli attacks on the city of Nabatieh and nearby villages in southern Lebanon. The Israeli military confirmed strikes against what it described as “ Hezbollah terrorist targets in southern Lebanon” after Hezbollah had “launched more than 50 projectiles at Israeli forces,” th

stories.title: Iran announces plans to close Strait of Hormuz, alleging ‘violations of the ceasefire’
stories.summary: Iran is again planning to close the Strait of Hormuz, according to an Iranian consulate and reports from Iranian state-owned media on Saturday, throwing yet another wrench into U.S. President Donald Trump’s plans to sell his peace agreement with Tehran to a skeptical electorate . Iran’s Consulate-General in India wrote on X on Saturday that the closure will be partly “in response to the continuous violations of the ceasefire in southern Lebanon” and will come as the “first step as a response to

stories.title: ‘They have all the power’: investigation finds that 93% of ICE arrests targeted Latinos
stories.summary: 430 ICE street arrests filed over a five-month period were clustered in predominantly Latino communities across New Jersey and New York Federal agents have arrested hundreds of immigrants off New York and New Jersey streets in recent months in a stealth enforcement campaign that disproportionately targeted people from Latin American countries, according to an investigation by the City Reporter based on a review of more than 1,200 lawsuits. More than 93% of the people grabbed off area streets who

stories.title: Trump loyalist Jim Jordan linked to group that received ‘dark money’ from ICE detention contractor
stories.summary: Report finds close ties between the Trump administration and Geo Group, which profits from anti-immigration crackdown Jim Jordan is among the most famous names in this stretch of Ohio. The congressman and chair of the powerful House judiciary committee is considered among the most conservative and influential members in Congress, and is a longtime loyalist of Donald Trump. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran says it is closing strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: Unclear if threat has been carried out or if move will jeopardise talks with US scheduled for Sunday Iran has said it is closing the strait of Hormuz after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon in a move that threatens to derail the fragile interim peace deal with the US, signed just days ago. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned ships not to approach the strategic waterway, which before the war carried a fifth of global oil and liquid gas supplies, citing what it called Israeli crimes in

stories.title: Meloni, Trump trade barbs in escalating spat after G7
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Saturday stuck hard to his claim that Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni “begged” for a photo with him at the G7 summit earlier this week, drawing a sharp retort from the Italian leader. “These constant, unprovoked attacks are senseless,” Meloni said after Trump’s latest broadside against her. The two right-wing leaders, once politically close , have been at loggerheads since Trump on Friday told Italian television channel La7 that

stories.title: Sánchez’s wife barred from leaving Spain ahead of corruption trial
stories.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife, Begoña Gómez, has been barred from leaving Spain and will be required to appear in court twice a month under a judge’s decision issued on Saturday. The measures were imposed as part of proceedings ahead of a corruption trial against Gómez and are intended to prevent any risk of flight before a verdict is reached. The case — which also names Gómez’s adviser Cristina Álvarez and businessman Juan Carlos Barrabés as defendants — involves

stories.title: Ten years on, has the Brexit vote helped or hindered the EU?
stories.summary: Some said Britain’s departure would bring down the union, but countries are still queueing to join On the night of 23 June 2016, a storm broke out over Brussels. Rain poured and lightning flashed over the European Union headquarters. The next day dawned grey and calm, but the political weather was raging. Britain had voted to leave the EU. Nigel Farage, then leader of the UK Independence party (Ukip), declared the EU “finished” and “dead”. France’s Marine Le Pen, the Netherlands’ Geert Wilders a

stories.title: US treasury chief urged Trump not to host ‘Mr Bean on crack’ Zelenskyy, book says
stories.summary: Suggestion that Scott Bessent so described a world leader included in Regime Change, by New York Times reporters Scott Bessent, the US treasury secretary, advised Donald Trump not to host Volodymyr Zelenskyy in the Oval Office, having called the Ukrainian president a “little fucker”, a “special-needs child” and “Mr Bean on crack”, according to a new book. The suggestion that a US cabinet official described a world leader in such terms is included in Regime Change, a blockbusting account of the s

stories.title: Burnham allies confident of No 10 ‘coronation’ after surge in backers
stories.summary: Some Labour MPs still want former mayor to face ‘scrutiny’ of ideas through contested leadership race Allies of Andy Burnham are increasingly confident of a coronation-style transfer of power after the number of MPs backing him for the Labour leadership surged following his byelection victory. Burnham and his team are understood to have spent the last few days enlisting the support of MPs and ministers, as he prepares to challenge Keir Starmer in the coming weeks. Continue reading...

stories.title: Counter-terrorism officers investigate after five injured in violent incidents in Edinburgh
stories.summary: Several of those hurt are reported to be Muslim as Police Scotland say 36-year-old white Scottish man arrested Counter-terrorism officers are involved in investigations into a series of violent incidents in Edinburgh that left five people injured, police said. The organisation Muslim Engagement and Development said several of those injured were Muslim. Continue reading...

stories.title: LAPD releases footage of moment officers shot and killed woman’s dog
stories.summary: Video also shows that during calm exchange before dog entered hallway, an officer pointed pistol directly at woman The Los Angeles police department has released footage of the moment when officers shot and killed a woman’s dog in the hallway outside her apartment in the Canoga Park neighborhood. Police had responded to reports of a woman screaming on 13 June, which turned out to be cheering, the night that the New York Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs to win the NBA finals. Continue readin

stories.title: Boats, bankers and borders: five symbols that sum up Brexit a decade on
stories.summary: What do the touchstones of the referendum debate tell us about the complex legacy of Britain leaving the EU? Ten years ago the UK voted 52% to 48% to leave the European Union, triggering a long and tortuous political process. It took until 1 January 2021 for the country to sever its links to the single market and customs union, but the fractures Brexit left in Britain’s body politic, international relations and economy remain. Continue reading...

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah renew ceasefire after deadly flareup disrupts opening of Iran talks
stories.summary: At least five killed in Israeli air strike in southern Lebanon on Saturday morning, as US envoy Steve Witkoff reportedly prepares to open talks with Iranian foreign minister What lessons will Iran’s new leadership draw from the 110-day war? ‘It’s a big mistake’: Israelis feel betrayed and angry after Iran peace deal Israel and Hezbollah agreed to renew a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon on Friday after 24 hours of intense violence that posed an early challenge to the new agreement between the US and

stories.title: Bolivia's Paz declares state of emergency over blockades
stories.summary: President Rodrigo Paz says he has declared the state of emergency "to free the country's roads." Blockades have become a major tactic in weeks of intensifying protests to demand that the president resign.

stories.title: India news: Hundreds attend 'Cockroach' party protest in New Delhi
stories.summary: The viral Cockroach Janta Party is escalating pressure on the government, citing exam irregularities and student suicides in its campaign against Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. DW has more.

stories.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
stories.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

stories.title: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
stories.summary: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
stories.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

stories.title: A viral doomsday scenario aims to shake Europe out of its AI complacency
stories.summary: Does a thought-experiment about US ascendancy in the technology say as much about AI jitters as it does about the reality? It’s 2031 and the US and China are about to tear Europe into pieces. The US ploughed vast sums into datacentres and the EU did not. China built robots and Europe did not. American companies “restructured” their workflows around AI and fired people, while EU workers went on long lunch breaks and handed over administrative tasks to the AI model Claude. Continue reading...

stories.title: What lessons will Iran’s new leadership draw from the 110-day war?
stories.summary: Now fighting is over, the question is how Iran’s government will behave. Early clues point to more authoritarianism and prioritising relations with China The precise ideological lessons that Iran’s new leadership draws from the 110-day war may prove to be the overriding factor in determining whether negotiations with the US culminate in an agreement that verifiably prevents the country from developing a nuclear weapon – an outcome that could usher in a new era for the Iranian economy while also

stories.title: Israeli attack kills famed turtle sanctuary ecologist in Lebanon
stories.summary: Mona Khalil led decades-long effort to protect nesting site for turtles near her home in south of the country The Lebanese marine activist Mona Khalil, who became a beloved figure in the country for a decades-long effort to protect a nesting site for turtles near her home, has died from injuries sustained in an Israeli strike. Khalil, 76, ran a sanctuary called the Orange House Project near the Mediterranean city of Tyre. She hosted volunteers in her house to clean and monitor a mile-long beach

stories.title: ‘Like a bomb had gone off’: suspected arson attack fuels Glastonbury unease
stories.summary: Arrests after explosion and caravan fires heighten controversy over number of people living in vehicles Jan Johnston was tucked up in the van she calls home when she was rocked by the explosion. “I heard this massive boom,” she said. “I came out and there was thick, black, billowing smoke. It was like a bomb had gone off.” It turned out not to be a bomb, but a suspected arson attack on one of the many caravans, motorhomes and converted vehicles tucked away in side roads and industrial estates ar

stories.title: Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote
stories.summary: Colombians will choose on Sunday between two men whose lives have been very differently shaped by the militias, and whose visions for the country are poles apart Whoever wins Sunday’s presidential runoff vote in Colombia, the country’s next leader will have a personal history intertwined with one of the criminal forces at the heart of a decades-long armed conflict that claimed nearly half a million lives. The lives of Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella have, in very different ways, been sh

stories.title: Louisiana pastor sentenced to 80 years for sexually molesting two boys
stories.summary: Terry Reed, found guilty of rape and molestation of a juvenile, called ‘utter failure of a man’ by victim’s mother A suburban New Orleans religious pastor has been sentenced to 80 years’ imprisonment after being convicted of sexually molesting two boys – the third time in which he was found guilty of abusing minors. While Terry Reed received his punishment at a state court hearing on Thursday, the mother of one of his survivors read a victim-impact statement on behalf of her son which called him

stories.title: Corbyn criticises ‘strange’ lack of policy in leadership debate and says Burnham must offer real change – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story here A losing streak? Makerfield shows mounting dangers for Nigel Farage From Restore and tactical voting to questions over that £5m gift, the Reform leader faces challenges on several fronts, writes senior political correspondent Peter Walker. As those around Nigel Farage are fond of pointing out, Reform UK has now led in more than 300 consecutive national polls. When it comes to byelections, though, it is fair to say the party’s results

stories.title: Nine people in critical condition after Bedford train crash kills driver
stories.summary: East of England ambulance service says number of people listed as seriously injured has increased to 32 Nine people are in a critical condition after the Bedford train crash that killed the driver of one of the trains, police have confirmed. The total number of people listed as seriously injured has increased to 32, East of England ambulance service said on Saturday. Continue reading...

stories.title: A Kennedy, a ‘nerd’, an ex-Republican and a tech target: New York races toward a House seat
stories.summary: In NY-12, four Democrats, including one Kennedy, are vying to replace Jerry Nadler – and potentially shake things up When news broke that a safely Democratic seat in New York’s wealthiest congressional district was becoming vacant, it was inevitable that there would be a crowded field of candidates. What people might not have expected is that the subsequent Democratic primary would become one of the country’s most closely watched and action-packed, the race coming to reflect a range of Democrats

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
stories.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

stories.title: Pakistan: Roadside bombs kill 7 in northwest
stories.summary: A police officer said twin blasts in Pakistan's northwestern Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province were triggered remotely. No group immediately claimed responsibility.

stories.title: Iran says it's closing Strait of Hormuz again
stories.summary: Iran's joint military command said the strait had been closed due to Israel's continued attacks in Lebanon. Meanwhile, Iran's negotiators are heading to Switzerland for Iran-US talks on their interim deal. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany news: Summer thunderstorms wreak havoc
stories.summary: Germany was lashed overnight by summer storms that caused flooding, injuries and evacuations. Lightning, high winds and heavy rain saw several people hospitalized. DW has the latest.

stories.title: After a successful Ukrainian drone attack, questions are being asked about Russia's air defenses
stories.summary: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Terror police probe suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
stories.summary: Counterterrorism police have taken charge after a series of violent attacks in the Scottish capital that left five men injured.

stories.title: Italian tourist dies in Dominican Republic resort blaze
stories.summary: Around 1,690 tourists were evacuated from a luxury resort in the village of Bayahibe after a fire broke out.

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
stories.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed again but traffic continues, according to monitoring
stories.summary: Tehran had linked its announcement to Israel carrying out a wave of fresh strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday morning.

stories.title: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
stories.summary: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

stories.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

stories.title: Tourist dies in Dominican Republic luxury resort fire
stories.summary: Nearly 1,700 people were evacuated from the huge blaze at Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach in Bayahibe, a popular resort town on the Caribbean coast.

stories.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
stories.summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died from her injuries after the Israeli strike.

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz will be closed over Israel attacks on Lebanon
stories.summary: Iran said Israel's continued attacks in Lebanon are a breach of Tehran's agreement with the US to end the war.

stories.title: 16 dead and 12 injured following Israeli strikes, Lebanon's civil defence agency says
stories.summary: It comes less than a day after US officials said Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah had agreed to a renewed ceasefire.

stories.title: 'All of Lebanon must burn,' Israeli minister says after IDF reports four soldiers killed
stories.summary: Israel's military said the soldiers were killed "in combat" when their tank was hit during an operation near Kfar Tebnit.

stories.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages
stories.summary: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

stories.title: Anthony Albanese says Australia’s first mainland case of deadly H5N1 bird flu ‘concerning’
stories.summary: Tests taken in Western Australia suggest a second bird was also infected, but there is no evidence poultry or agriculture systems are affected Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The deadly H5N1 bird flu strain has arrived on the Australian mainland with test results confirming a migratory seabird found on the Western Australian coast was positive for the disease. The agriculture minister, Julie Collins, confirmed a brown skua – found unwell last Sunday at Cape Le Grand

stories.title: Fears doctors criticising Israel may be silenced as health watchdog adopts contested antisemitism definition
stories.summary: Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency to use International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance definition for ‘regulatory work’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s health watchdog has adopted a contested definition of antisemitism to guide its regulatory work. The move has been welcomed by the nation’s peak Jewish body while the Australia Palestine Advocacy Network (Apan) has warned it could silence health professionals from criticising Israel’s human r

stories.title: ‘It’s a big mistake’: Israelis feel betrayed and angry after Iran peace deal
stories.summary: In ‘middle Israel’ there are fears Iran could rebuild stronger – and there is particular ire for Donald Trump In the Tree brasserie off Herzl Street in Rehovot, there was much that almost everyone agreed on. Few contested that the ceasefire deal concluded by Iran and the US a few days earlier was very bad for Israel. “We were betrayed by President Trump,” said Avi Perez, 55. They believed, too, that Israel, more than ever, was surrounded by danger that it would have to confront alone. “It is str

stories.title: Beyond the beach: Spain pushes offbeat regions as tourist numbers nudge 100m
stories.summary: Exclusive: Tourism minister says another likely record year of visitor growth is not a worry amid move to welcome tourists out of season and market less frequented areas Spain is redoubling its efforts to push its tourist appeal beyond the familiar “sun and sand and coast” model as it prepares for another record-breaking year in which the number of foreign visitors could reach 100 million for the first time, the country’s tourism minister has said. Speaking to the Guardian, Jordi Hereu rejected

stories.title: UK: Train collision north of London kills at least one person
stories.summary: At least one person died and many others were injured in the train collision about 90 kilometers north of London.

stories.title: Meloni 'stunned' by Trump's comment she 'begged' for photo
stories.summary: Donald Trump's comments that Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni "begged for a picture" have elicited a furious response from Rome. Meloni said she was "stunned."

stories.title: Trump unveils interim Air Force One, a gift from Qatar
stories.summary: A former Qatar-owned jumbo jet, converted into the new presidential aircraft, is nearly ready for service. Trump said it had been transformed into "a flying White House."

stories.title: First case of H5 bird flu confirmed in Australia
stories.summary: A migratory seabird tested positive for the contagious H5 variant of avian influenza in Western Australia. Australia had been the only continent where H5 was not detected.

stories.title: Woman killed, hundreds evacuate in Dominican Republic hotel fire
stories.summary: A huge fire tore through a four-star beach resort in the Dominican Republic, killing one woman. About 1,700 tourists were evacuated as the fire appeared to destroy most of the hotel.

stories.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
stories.summary: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

stories.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
stories.summary: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

stories.title: James Burrows, legendary director of Cheers and Friends, dies aged 85
stories.summary: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

stories.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
stories.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

stories.title: Tömegek araszolnak a Balaton felé az autópályán
stories.summary: A kánikula elől menekülnek a tóhoz, így már reggel 9-kor telítődtek a sávok.

stories.title: „50 meccsenként egyszer történik ilyen” – a török kapitány nem érti, hogyan estek ki két meccs után a vb-ről
stories.summary: Vincenzo Montella szerint a sors nem velük volt.

stories.title: A mozdonyvezető meghalt, 33-an súlyosan megsérültek a pénteki angliai vonatbalesetben
stories.summary: Az egyik vonat a nyílt pályán megállt, a mögötte érkező vonat hátulról nekiütközött.

stories.title: A 16 éves dédunokája ölhette meg a 85 éves nőt Józsefvárosban
stories.summary: A kihallgatás után őrizetbe vették a fiút. Párját, egy 15 éves lányt bűnpártolással gyanúsítják.

stories.title: Elindult a MÁV-nál a nyári főszezoni menetrend
stories.summary: A Balatonnál kétszer annyi vonat közlekedik, így közel háromszorosára nő az ülőhelyek száma.

stories.title: Júliustól drágulnak a bankszámlák, egy jó választás tízezreket érhet
stories.summary: Teljesen fölöslegesen ki szeretne akár száz forintot is kiadni? Pedig milliók teszik ezt nap mint nap a bankszámláikkal, holott egy kis odafigyeléssel akár a saját bankunknál is tudnánk csökkenteni a költségeinket olcsóbb vagy akár díjmentes csomaggal és a fölösleges dolgok lemondásával. Júliustól a bankok többsége árat emel, de közben a számláját hozzájuk vivő ügyfeleknek akár százezer forintot is adnak. Így lehet spórolni a bankoláson és kihasználni az ajándékpénzeket.

stories.title: Agyrázkódás gyanújával terült el, aztán gólt fejelt – szakértők szerint a FIFA nem elég óvatos a fejsérülésekkel
stories.summary: A FIFA szerint az orvosi stáboknak minden lehetőségük adott a pontos kivizsgáláshoz.

stories.title: Megnyert egy időközi választást, bevonulhat Londonba átvenni a miniszterelnöki posztot
stories.summary: Régóta inog Keir Starmer brit kormányfő széke, de a Munkáspárton belül óriási támogatottságot élvező Andy Burnham eddig nem hívhatta ki, mivel nem volt parlamenti képviselő. Starmer kitartana, de könnyen lehet, hogy hamar új vezetője lesz az Egyesült Királyságnak.

stories.title: Meghalt az autó sofőrje, miután menetrend szerinti busznak ütközött Csetényben
stories.summary: A buszon nyolcan sérültek meg.

stories.title: Bécstől nem messze olyan háborítatlan vadonban túráztunk, amiből már csak kevés van Európában
stories.summary: Magashegyi tájak, csipkés bércek, nyári hófoltok, hangulatos hütték, via ferrata – elsőre a legtöbb embernek efféle képek ugranak be, ha azt hallja, „ausztriai túra”. Az viszont, hogy legfeljebb Mátra magasságú hegyeken vízesésdömpinggel, érintetlen vadonnal vagy ritka állatokkal lehet találkozni, vélhetően pont annyira új másoknak is, mint nekünk volt egészen addig, míg el nem kirándultunk a Bécstől bő másfél órára lévő Waldviertelbe. Jó eséllyel Közép-Európa legvadregényesebb tájain túráztunk

stories.title: Lázgörcsöt kapott és meghalt egy kétéves gyerek a tatabányai kórházban
stories.summary: A család feljelentést tett, a rendőrség nyomozást indított.

stories.title: Az elektromos rollerek 20-25 km/h-s sebességkorlátozását írná elő Vitézy Dávid a KRESZ-ben
stories.summary: Júliusban megkezdődhet az új KRESZ társadalmi egyeztetése, és várhatóan már az ősszel a kormány elé kerül a jogszabály. Az elektromos rollerek használatát biztosan szigorítják.

stories.title: Az egyik legősibb fagyi tojás nélkül lesz szuperkrémes
stories.summary: Nem az olasz gelato a legrégebbi tejes fagylalt, hanem a kulfi, amihez hosszú órákon át kell elpárologtatni a tej víztartalmát, cserébe iszonyú jó. A hosszú órák pedig több módon is lerövidíthetők.

stories.title: Meghalt a Jóbarátok és az Agymenők rendezője
stories.summary: Az amerikai szitkomok legendás alakja, James Burrows 85 éves volt. A Jóbarátokban Ross karakterét játszó David Schwimmer szerint a rendező minden színészből a legjobbat hozta ki.

stories.title: Annyira nincs víz a Velencei-tóban, hogy több strand ki sem nyit idén
stories.summary: A Velencei-tó vízszintje napok óta a történelmi mélypont, 53 cm közelében mozog, szombaton 54 centimétert jegyeztek fel.

stories.title: „Az a baj, hogy öregszem, és felejtek” – demencia elleni harc a szentendrei Skanzenben
stories.summary: A demens idősek régi tárgyak, fényképek és dalok segítségével újra kapcsolatba lépnek saját múltjukkal, ezzel lassítják a feledés folyamatát.

stories.title: Megnéztük, mennyire éri meg strandolni idén, a hekk meghekkelte a pénztárcánkat
stories.summary: Hiába szisszenünk fel a sajtos-tejfölös lángos árától, összességében nem olyan vészes a strandhelyzet, mint elsőre látszik.

stories.title: Melléthei-Barna Márton abszurdnak tartja a Fidesz vádját, nem kötött paktumot az alkotmánybírókkal
stories.summary: A tiszás képviselő szerint az alkotmánybírók szakmai okok miatt döntöttek úgy, hogy kizáratják magukat a köztársasági elnök ügyéből. További jó vergődést kívánt a Fidesznek.

stories.summary: Az Integritás Hatóság elnöke szerint ezermilliárdos összeg ment el a sokat kritizált és vizsgálat alatt álló programra, ám a nyilvános források nem mutatnak ekkora költést.

stories.title: Repülőgép-balesetben meghalt az Ubisoft egyik alapítója
stories.summary: Claude Guillemot egy kisrepülőgépet vezetett, ami egy mezőre zuhant. A gép mindkét utasa meghalt.

stories.summary: Idén június 21-én, vasárnap 10 óra 24 perckor lesz a nyári napforduló.

stories.summary: A 34 éves brazil csatár már három éve nem volt válogatott, mégis kivitték a világbajnokságra.

Országos közlekedésszervező cég és független örökségvédelmi hivatal állhat fel, és 5 százalékra csökkentenék a tűzifa áfáját.

stories.summary: A köztársasági elnök szerint a Tisza 16 hét alatt nagyobb hatalomkoncentrációt akar elérni, mint a Fidesz 16 év alatt.

Terrorelhárítási osztag nyomoz az Edinburgh-ban történt támadások ügyében

stories.summary: Rengeteg eszközünk lenne egy rossz kapcsolatban a megcsaláson kívül, de az vajon rendben van, ha lájkolgatom az exem képeit? Hogy működik a micro-cheating, és miért ilyen populáris kapcsolati forma a situationship? Videós interjú Knapek Éva szakpszichológussal.

stories.summary: Hollandia felmosta a padlót a kétarcú svéd csapattal.

Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Cuba's President Miguel Díaz-Canel admits 'urgent changes' needed to overcome crisis
stories.summary: The oil blockade imposed by President Donald Trump in January has brought the island's already moribund economy to the brink of collapse, marked by power cuts sometimes lasting over 30 hours.

stories.title: Mangione's lawyers reverse course on psychiatric defence in state murder trial

stories.summary: His fierce defence of the Iran plan amid mounting criticism comes as speculation intensifies about a possible 2028 presidential run.

The explosion was caused by a Ukrainian drone attack where one of Moscow’s most important energy facilities was damaged for the second time this week Moscow oil refinery struck in Ukraine’s biggest air raid on city since start of war Continue reading...

stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...

stories.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with

The brown skua, a subantarctic species, was found at Cape Le Grand national park on Sunday a

stories.title: 'Neither I nor Italy ever beg': Giorgia Meloni rejects Trump's claim she begged for a photo – video
stories.summary: The Italian prime minister posted a video on X saying that Donald Trump's claim she begged him for a photo was 'completely made up'. I wouldn’t have taken it, but I felt sorry for her' Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
stories.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

stories.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

stories.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
stories.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
stories.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

stories.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

stories.summary: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

stories.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect

stories.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
stories.summary: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

stories.summary: Hegseth slammed Europe for prioritising what he called liberal ideals over practical defence needs as he lambasted the alliance’s defence chiefs in a rare appearance at the NATO HQ in Brussels.

stories.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
stories.summary: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

stories.summary: The royals were always popular, but Crown Princess Mette-Marit’s Epstein connections and her son’s rape convictions have profoundly shaken public trust The Norwegian monarchy is in crisis. stories.summary: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the traffick

stories.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

stories.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
stories.summary: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

stories.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
stories.summary: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

stories.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
stories.summary: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

stories.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
stories.summary: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

stories.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
stories.summary: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones were launched towards an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

stories.title: Humanoid robots and smart homes put AI centre stage at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
stories.summary: France is putting artificial intelligence and robotics in the spotlight at VivaTech 2026, which opened on 17 June at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles.

stories.title: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds
stories.summary: Review reveals rise in users and rates of psychosis in countries where cannabis is sold commercially Decriminalising the possession of cannabis or strictly regulating access to the drug do not appear to drive up usage, but when the drug is sold commercially the number of users increases and more mental health problems are seen, a review has found. An international team analysed the dramatic shift in policies on cannabis between 2000 and 2025, including how the numbers of people taking the drug,

stories.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
stories.summary: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Cynical to get power’: Michel Barnier on Boris Johnson, Brexit and the EU’s future
stories.summary: Former negotiator believes in an unstable world, it is ‘perfectly possible’ the UK can rejoin the EU with old opt-outs UK could keep special pre-Brexit terms if it rejoined EU, Michel Barnier says A couple of years ago, Michel Barnier spent a weekend with Boris Johnson’s father, Stanley. It was not some ghoulish Brexit spin-off of The Traitors, but the result of the former EU negotiator’s wife, Isabelle, being a close friend of Johnson’s French cousin, Anne du Boucheron, the owner of Château de

stories.title: Nathaniel Brown: The left back Germany have been waiting for
stories.summary: A strong option at left back is overdue for Germany. Nathaniel Brown is quickly becoming a star at the World Cup.

stories.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
stories.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

stories.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
stories.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
stories.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

stories.title: Trump welcomed to Versailles for dinner with Macron
stories.summary: Donald Trump was welcomed to the Palace of Versailles on Wednesday evening for a private reception and dinner hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron following the conclusion of the G7 summit in Evian.

stories.title: 'We had to get out of the way': The backlash over delivery robots
stories.summary: As the delivery vehicles increasing take to US streets, bans and protest groups are springing up.

stories.title: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
stories.summary: A cemetery at the epicentre of the outbreak is rapidly filling up, however large traditional funerals have been scrapped.

stories.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
stories.summary: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

stories.title: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
stories.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

stories.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Palace of Versailles
stories.summary: The 14-point Memorandum of Understanding was signed at a post-G7 dinner in France.

stories.title: Trump signs initial deal to end Iran war and open Strait of Hormuz as nuclear talks continue
stories.summary: Trump signed an initial framework deal to restore the pre-war status quo between Iran and the United States, inking an initial agreement towards ending a war he started with Israel on 28 February.

stories.title: Moment Trump signs US-Iran deal at Palace of Versailles
stories.summary: Video released by the French presidency shows Donald Trump signing an agreement aimed at ending the war with Iran during a dinner at Versailles, with French President Emmanuel Macron seated beside him.

stories.title: UK: Tommy Robinson’s Oxford Union debate sparks protests and free speech backlash
stories.summary: Tommy Robinson’s appearance at the Oxford Union prompts large protests and reignites the UK debate over free expression and platforms for extremists.

stories.title: Watch: Moment Trump signs US-Iran agreement at Versailles
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran at the Palace of Versailles on 18 June, marking a significant diplomatic step aimed at easing the Middle East conflict that has unsettled the region since February.

stories.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.
